mission: M1-T1 silesia fastloop load-latency axis: strengthen next-litlen-entry preload (break refill->load serialization)
change: match-tail: load next litlen entry from CURRENT (pre-refill) bitbuf THEN refill (mirrors libdeflate decompress_template.h:571-572); + subtable-offset guard refill to hold bitsleft>=LITLEN_TABLEBITS(11) so the pre-refill preload is byte-identical
mechanism_disasm: base: entry-load index depends on refill orr (and x,x17[refilled bitbuf],#0x7ff; ldr [x4,x]) -> load latency serialized behind refill. variant: and x,x19[pre-refill bitbuf],#0x7ff; ldr [x4,x]; ldr [x10,x9](refill) -> entry load address independent of refill; two loads issue in parallel.

byte_exact.result: base==var==gunzip-oracle on all corpora T1+T4; cargo test inflate+decompress+proptest all pass; make quick pass

wall_gate.tool: fulcrum vs (fulcrum-mac feat/critpath-libdeflate dc0355e), steady kpc, /dev/null sink, sudo

base_over_libdeflate.verdict: NOT-RESOLVABLE this session (base ~= libdeflate; prior 1.014 finding-decayed)

conclusion: Strengthening the next-litlen-entry preload (load-before-refill, address independent of the refill) reduces the M1-T1 silesia wall by ~5% and moves gz/libdeflate from ~parity to 0.9487 (below 1.014, below 1.0). Byte-exact, gated, ParallelSM.
scope_owed: Gate-3 cross-arch: x86_64 + M4 replication owed (change is arch-neutral source; on x86_64 the default hot path is the asm-kernel, so effect there is unverified).
